metadata.description: SmartPatch Authentication Controls — production-grade schema (AUTH-01 .. AUTH-04). Each control contains enforcement commands, validation checks, failure modes, privileges and authoritative references.
metadata.author: SmartPatch Team (converted)

controls.name: Disable LM hash storage
controls.description: Prevent storage of LAN Manager (LM) password hashes. LM hashes are weak and enable easy offline cracking; disable storage so new password changes do not store LM hashes.
controls.rationale: Preventing LM hash storage mitigates use of legacy weak hashes for offline credential attacks; existing LM hashes remain until passwords are changed.

os_applicability.notes: Older pre-Windows-7 systems may behave differently; password rotation required to remove existing LM hashes.

enforcement_methods.notes: Set NoLMHash = 1 to prevent storage of LM hashes for future password changes. Existing LM hashes remain until users change passwords.

enforcement_methods.description: Use baseline policies and stage password change campaign; enforce via configuration management.
enforcement_methods.notes: There is no single GPO toggle that retroactively removes existing LM hashes; plan password resets for accounts with LM present.

validation_checks.command: Audit: extract hashes from NTDS.dit/LSA secrets with safe-forensic tooling in a lab, or verify via vendor tooling that LM hashes are absent after password change.
validation_checks.success_criteria: No account returns a stored LM hash; only NT hash stored.

failure_modes: Setting NoLMHash prevents future LM hashes but does not remove existing LM hashes — require password resets.
Some very old legacy authentication tools may expect LM behavior; test critical legacy services.
controls.functional_impact: Low (affects only password storage for future changes); operational impact occurs during password reset cadence.

priority.priority_reason: Eliminates a long-standing weak storage mechanism useful to attackers.

audit_query_examples: Post-change verification: confirm no LM hash present in forensic/ID store (lab/test)

references.title: Prevent Windows from storing a LAN Manager (LM) hash of the user's password

controls.implementation_notes: After setting NoLMHash, schedule and track password resets for privileged accounts and service accounts. Monitor for applications that rely on legacy hashes.

controls.name: Enforce NTLMv2 only (LmCompatibilityLevel = 5)
controls.description: Require NTLMv2 and refuse LM/NTLMv1 to harden authentication protocol negotiation.
controls.rationale: NTLMv1 and LM are weak; setting LmCompatibilityLevel to 5 ensures clients send NTLMv2 responses only and servers refuse LM/NTLMv1.

os_applicability.notes: Domain controllers and domain-level compatibility must be aligned; test legacy systems.

enforcement_methods.notes: Value 5 = Send NTLMv2 response only. Can also be configured via Group Policy: Computer Configuration → Windows Settings → Security Settings → Local Policies → Security Options → 'Network security: LAN Manager authentication level'.

enforcement_methods.description: Deploy via GPO to ensure consistent domain-wide policy; align domain controllers and legacy systems before enforcing.

validation_checks.command: Use network authentication test tools or test clients to confirm server accepts only NTLMv2; review security event logs for NTLM fallback/denied events.
validation_checks.success_criteria: Authentication attempts use NTLMv2 and legacy LM/NTLMv1 attempts are refused or fail.

failure_modes: Legacy devices/applications that only support NTLMv1 will fail to authenticate — inventory & vendor testing required.
Misaligned domain controller settings can cause authentication issues for legacy accounts.
controls.functional_impact: Medium — may break legacy authentication for unsupported systems.

priority.priority_reason: Prevents downgrade attacks and use of weak authentication; aligns with Microsoft hardening guidance.

audit_query_examples: Check security logs and test authentication from representative legacy and current clients

references.title: Network security: LAN Manager authentication level (explain values and guidance)
Active Directory hardening guidance: Disabling NTLMv1

controls.implementation_notes: Run phased rollout: inventory NTLM usage, pilot on non-critical hosts, then enforce in waves. Provide exception handling for legacy systems or isolate them on a management VLAN.

controls.name: UAC hardening (EnableLUA and related policies)
controls.description: Ensure User Account Control (UAC) is enabled and configured to require consent/elevation for administrative actions.
controls.rationale: UAC limits silent elevation and reduces the attack surface for privilege escalation by requiring explicit consent for administrative operations.

os_applicability.notes: Settings and registry paths vary slightly by OS; default recommended is EnableLUA = 1.

enforcement_methods.notes: EnableLUA = 1 turns on UAC. Additional related settings (ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin, PromptOnSecureDesktop) should be configured per baseline.

enforcement_methods.description: Use Administrative Templates / Security Options to configure UAC policies centrally.

validation_checks.command: Perform a privileged operation (installer or registry change) and verify the UAC consent prompt appears as configured.
validation_checks.success_criteria: UAC prompt behavior matches baseline (consent/elevation required).

failure_modes: Disabling UAC (EnableLUA=0) reduces security significantly; some legacy applications may require it off — prefer application remediation over turning UAC off.
Some privileged service behaviours differ under UAC; test service accounts and installers.
controls.functional_impact: Low to Medium depending on configured prompt behavior.

priority.priority_reason: Core OS security control; enabling UAC is a baseline requirement.

audit_query_examples: Test elevation prompts or use gpresult to validate applied GPO settings
controls.implementation_notes: Configure UAC in Audit/Pilot first if many legacy apps exist; also set related consent/prompt policies to fit operational needs. Reboot may be required for changes to take full effect.

controls.name: Restrict cached logons (CachedLogonsCount)
controls.description: Limit or disable cached domain logons to mitigate risk of offline credential use on lost or stolen endpoints.
controls.rationale: Reducing the number of cached logons limits the pool of locally-stored credential material available for offline attacks; in high-risk scenarios set to 0 to disable caching.

os_applicability.notes: Mobile and laptop users are impacted; plan exceptions for known offline usage.

enforcement_methods.notes: Set CachedLogonsCount to a string value between '0' and '50'. '0' disables caching for new logons; existing cached credentials are unaffected until removed.

enforcement_methods.description: Enforce via policy (e.g., Azure Guest Configuration or GPO) and document exceptions for laptop/mobile profiles.

validation_checks.success_criteria: Value exists and equals the configured limit (e.g., '0').

validation_checks.command: Simulate domain controller unavailability and attempt local logon with a cached account to confirm behavior.
validation_checks.success_criteria: Offline logon fails if cached logons disabled; if set to N, only N unique users may log on offline.

failure_modes: Setting to 0 prevents mobile users from logging on offline — may block legitimate use cases; coordinate with asset owners.
Existing cached credentials are not removed by changing the value; additional steps required to clear caches on devices in-scope.
controls.functional_impact: High for mobile/laptop users; medium for static desktops.

priority.priority_reason: Reduces offline credential exposure but operational impact on remote users must be managed.

audit_query_examples: Pilot test offline logon behavior on representative laptops

references.title: Cached domain logon information (CachedLogonsCount) — Microsoft documentation
STIG guidance: limit cached logon credentials on high-value systems

controls.implementation_notes: For enterprise rollout: create exception list for known offline users, run a pilot, and document steps to clear existing cached credentials where required. Reboot required for some changes.

source_references_loadbearing.claim: NoLMHash registry controls LM hash storage
LmCompatibilityLevel values and recommendation to use '5' for NTLMv2-only
EnableLUA UAC registry behavior and recommended enabled state
STIG/CIS guidance enforces cached logon limits and LM/NTLM hardening for high-value systems
